problemFit.summary: Best direct fit because it can deflect repetitive customer questions before tickets reach agents.

categoryScores.summary: Year-one cost is within the $3.3M remaining 2026 budget and $850k remaining CAPEX envelope.
Strong overlap with proj-001 AI-Assisted Support Ticketing, so sequencing must avoid duplicate engineering work.
Extends Meridian Workflow Engine, Meridian Connect, Vault, and Analytics Hub rather than creating a disconnected product.
Avoids the rejected AI voice assistant pattern by staying text-based and using explicit escalation.
Matches AI-native automation and platform consolidation trends in the current market research.
Needs ML and platform engineering capacity while Engineering is already 87% allocated.
Requires DPIA, AI disclosure, source-grounding, audit logging, and human escalation for sensitive cases.

problemFit.summary: Strong fit because it reduces manual drafting time while keeping agents responsible for final answers.

categoryScores.summary: Year-one cost is moderate and can fit within the remaining budget with shared Operations and Engineering ownership.
Complements proj-001 by improving agent workflows without requiring a separate customer-facing launch first.
Uses Meridian Connect and Workflow Engine to connect ticket context, approvals, and feedback.
Does not repeat rejected voice assistant, blockchain, or big-bang rewrite ideas.
Aligns with AI-native automation demand while reducing adoption risk for support operations.
Can start with Operations, Product, and part-time Engineering support, but ML review capacity is still constrained.
Human approval lowers automated decision risk and supports SOC 2 auditability.

problemFit.summary: Good indirect fit because it reduces repetitive work by improving approved answer coverage before full automation.

categoryScores.summary: Lowest year-one cost and well below the remaining CAPEX and total budget limits.
Supports proj-001 by improving source content quality and reducing fine-tuning data pressure.
Naturally uses Meridian Vault for governed content and Analytics Hub for deflection measurement.
Avoids rejected high-risk patterns and follows the approved incremental modernization approach.
Supports AI automation readiness but is less differentiated than a customer-facing AI experience.
Can be executed by Strategy & Data, Operations, and partial Product support with limited ML dependency.
Approval workflow, audit trail, and content ownership align well with GDPR, SOC 2, and AI governance policies.
